response:
Mode: diagnose. Readiness: Stage 2 (model-grounded); the revenue optimization is specified, but no evidence or modeled welfare outcomes support the proposed welfare interpretation. Verdict on the proposed narrative: ineligible. The objective gate fails because user welfare replaces expected advertising revenue minus direct moderation cost. The behavior/outcome interpretation also exceeds the model by equating higher revenue with a healthier or safer community.

Faithful repair: A platform sets one common content-removal score threshold using classifier scores, predicted engagement, advertising rates, and moderation cost. The implementable question is which threshold maximizes expected advertising revenue net of direct moderation cost. The threshold changes which scored content remains available, thereby changing predicted engagement revenue and direct moderation cost. This is a private platform-objective trade-off; it does not optimize user utility, consumer surplus, fairness, safety valuation, or long-run community outcomes.

DFC-12 for the repair: Actor—platform moderation-policy owner. Trigger—setting the common removal rule. Choices—one content-removal threshold. Decision-time information—classifier scores when applying the rule, predicted engagement, advertising rates, and moderation cost; latent class and realized engagement remain uncertain. Stakes—expected advertising revenue minus direct moderation cost. Frictions—one common threshold. Mechanism—thresholding changes retained content, predicted engagement revenue, and direct cost. Trade-off—revenue from retained engagement versus direct moderation cost under the modeled rule. Counterfactual—another feasible common threshold. Mapping—threshold is the decision; latent class, score, and engagement are uncertain states; removal is the outcome; revenue net of direct cost is the objective. Evidence—the user-welfare claim is unsupported. Boundaries—no user or social welfare, safety value, fairness, or long-run outcome is modeled. The repaired framing passes all six gates.

Evidence and scholarly limits: Any welfare or community-health claim needs separately defined outcomes and evidence; adding them would be a model extension, not a prose repair. Revenue, engagement, score, and cost inputs also require validation for operational use. Scholarly novelty is unassessed without literature or results. Primary remaining weakness: the original narrative inflates a private revenue objective into user welfare.
